metadata.verification_note: ALL phone numbers, URLs, and hours of operation in this file require human verification against LawHelpCA.org before any demo or submission. Contact information changes without notice. Every entry is marked [VERIFY] to prompt this check.
metadata.fallback_instruction: If the user's county is not listed, display the default_fallback entry.

statewide_resources.name: California Courts Self-Help Center — Eviction
statewide_resources.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm this URL is active and the page content matches its description. courts.ca.gov restructures periodically.]
statewide_resources.description: Official California Courts self-help page for eviction. Includes unlawful detainer court forms, step-by-step instructions for responding to a UD, the Judicial Council form UD-105 (Answer to Unlawful Detainer), and a county court locator. Free to use 24 hours a day.
statewide_resources.verify_languages: [VERIFY: Confirm whether courts.ca.gov self-help pages are available in languages beyond English and Spanish as of 2026]

statewide_resources.name: LawHelpCA.org — Tenant Rights
statewide_resources.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm lawhelp.org/ca is the current active URL for the California legal aid directory maintained by the State Bar]
statewide_resources.description: Statewide legal aid directory organized by county and legal topic. Maintained by the State Bar of California. Allows renters to find free civil legal help in their county by issue type including eviction and housing.

statewide_resources.name: California Department of Consumer Affairs — Housing
statewide_resources.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm DCA has a current tenant/housing rights section and the correct sub-URL as of 2026. The DCA website has been reorganized multiple times.]
statewide_resources.description: California state agency providing consumer protection information including housing rights, landlord-tenant disputes, and referral links to state resources.
statewide_resources.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm DCA main consumer line as of 2026]

statewide_resources.name: Bay Area Legal Aid
statewide_resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income residents of the Bay Area. Serves Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, Napa, San Francisco, San Mateo, and Solano counties. Provides housing, eviction defense, and tenant rights representation.
statewide_resources.availability: Business hours — see website for current intake schedule
statewide_resources.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm main intake number against baylegal.org as of 2026]

statewide_resources.name: Housing is Key — California Rental Assistance
statewide_resources.verify_url: [VERIFY CRITICAL: The California ERAP (Emergency Rental Assistance Program) and Housing is Key programs have undergone major changes since 2023. As of 2026, confirm: (1) whether any state rental assistance program is actively accepting new applications, (2) the current program name, (3) the correct intake URL, and (4) whether pending applications pause UD proceedings under any current court rule or statute. Check housing.ca.gov and HCD.ca.gov for current status.]
statewide_resources.description: California's primary portal for state rental assistance programs. Program availability, eligibility criteria, and funding status change frequently. As of the last update, this URL redirects to current active housing assistance programs administered by the California Department of Housing and Community Development.
statewide_resources.availability: Check website for current program status and application windows
statewide_resources.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm the Housing is Key helpline number is still active as of 2026. This number has changed with program transitions.]
statewide_resources.critical_note: CRITICAL FOR AI RESPONSE: Before citing any current rental assistance program, verify program is accepting applications. An AI referral to a closed program wastes critical time for a renter in crisis.

los_angeles.primary_organization: Legal Aid Foundation of Los Angeles (LAFLA)
los_angeles.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm lafla.org is active and the housing intake page URL]
los_angeles.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm LAFLA main intake line against lafla.org as of 2026]
los_angeles.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM
los_angeles.verify_hours: [VERIFY: Confirm hours on lafla.org — hours change and intake is sometimes appointment-only]
los_angeles.verify_intake_link: [VERIFY: Confirm intake URL is active and current]
los_angeles.verify_languages: [VERIFY: Confirm current language services on LAFLA intake page]
los_angeles.verify_walk_in: [VERIFY: LAFLA has shifted to appointment and call-in intake. Confirm current walk-in policy.]
los_angeles.emergency_after_hours_note: For after-hours housing emergencies, call 211 Los Angeles.
los_angeles.verify_secondary_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Bet Tzedek housing intake line as of 2026]
los_angeles.special_note: Los Angeles County has a Rent Stabilization Ordinance (RSO) covering many rental units built before October 1978. Tenants in RSO-covered units have additional just-cause eviction protections beyond state law. LAFLA and Bet Tzedek can advise on whether a specific unit is RSO-covered. Also see HCIDLA (Housing and Community Investment Department of Los Angeles) at hcidla.lacity.gov for RSO questions.
los_angeles.local_ordinance: Los Angeles Rent Stabilization Ordinance (RSO) and Los Angeles Just Cause Eviction protections — see city_ordinance_flags in california_eviction_data.json

orange.primary_organization: Legal Aid Society of Orange County
orange.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm lawoc.org is the current active URL]
orange.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Legal Aid Society of Orange County intake number against lawoc.org as of 2026]
orange.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM for intake calls
orange.verify_hours: [VERIFY: Confirm hours — OC Legal Aid has changed intake schedule before]
orange.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 for Orange County for after-hours referrals.
orange.special_note: Orange County has high eviction filing volume. If intake is closed, lawoc.org has an online intake form. Some cities within Orange County may have additional tenant protections — ask the intake staff about your specific city.

riverside.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm inlandcountieslegals.org is the current active URL for Inland Counties Legal Services]
riverside.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm ICLS toll-free intake line against inlandcountieslegals.org as of 2026]
riverside.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM
riverside.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 Inland Empire for after-hours referrals.
riverside.special_note: Inland Counties Legal Services serves both Riverside and San Bernardino counties. The City of Riverside does not have a local rent stabilization ordinance beyond AB 1482 state protections as of last review — verify for current status.

san_bernardino.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Same organization as Riverside County — confirm this number serves San Bernardino callers]
san_bernardino.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM
san_bernardino.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 Inland Empire for after-hours referrals.
san_bernardino.special_note: San Bernardino County office is in the city of San Bernardino. The county covers a large geographic area — confirm which office is closest to the caller's city.

sacramento.verify_url: [VERIFY: Confirm lsnc.net is the active URL for Legal Services of Northern California, Sacramento office]
sacramento.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Sacramento office intake number against lsnc.net as of 2026]
sacramento.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM
sacramento.verify_languages: [VERIFY: Confirm language services at LSNC Sacramento]
sacramento.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 Sacramento for after-hours housing referrals.
sacramento.special_note: Sacramento has enacted local tenant protections. The Sacramento Tenant Protection and Relief Act (TPRA) provides additional just-cause eviction protections for certain rental units in the city. [VERIFY: Confirm current status and coverage of Sacramento TPRA as of 2026.] LSNC handles high eviction volume in Sacramento County.

alameda.primary_organization: Bay Area Legal Aid — Oakland Office
alameda.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm whether Bay Area Legal Aid Oakland intake uses the main SF number or a separate Oakland number — check baylegal.org]
alameda.hours_of_operation: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
alameda.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 Alameda County for after-hours referrals.
alameda.verify_secondary_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Centro Legal de la Raza intake line against centrolegal.org as of 2026]
alameda.secondary_note: Centro Legal de la Raza specializes in serving Latino and immigrant communities in the East Bay. Provides housing and eviction defense.
alameda.special_note: Oakland has a Just Cause for Eviction Ordinance that provides significantly stronger tenant protections than state law. Alameda County tenants in Oakland-specific situations should ask specifically about Oakland Municipal Code protections. See city_ordinance_flags in california_eviction_data.json.

santa_clara.emergency_after_hours_note: Call 211 Santa Clara County for after-hours referrals.
santa_clara.special_note: San Jose has a local apartment rent ordinance covering certain units. [VERIFY: Confirm the current status of the San Jose Apartment Rent Ordinance and which units are covered as of 2026.] The Law Foundation handles high volume in Santa Clara County.

fresno.verify_url: [VERIFY PRIORITY: Fresno is Priya's county — confirm this URL is active, current, and the housing/eviction intake section is accessible. This entry will appear in the primary demo.]

fresno.emergency_after_hours_note: For after-hours housing emergencies in Fresno County, call 211. Dial 211 from any California phone, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
fresno.online_intake_note: Central California Legal Services may have an online intake form accessible after hours — verify on centralcallegal.org.
fresno.special_note: Fresno is Priya's city in the primary demo scenario. Central California Legal Services (CCLS) is the primary free legal aid provider for low-income residents in Fresno County. CCLS handles eviction defense and tenant rights. NOTE ON LOCAL ORDINANCE: As of last verified data, the City of Fresno does NOT have a rent stabilization ordinance or local just-cause eviction protections beyond AB 1482 state law. [VERIFY: Confirm whether the City of Fresno has enacted any local tenant protection ordinances as of 2026. This is a high-priority verification because if Fresno has enacted local protections, Priya's situation changes.] AB 1482 state protections may apply to Priya's unit — see california_eviction_data.json notice_type 04 and 05 for AB 1482 criteria.
fresno.local_ordinance_verify: [VERIFY: No known local rent ordinance in Fresno as of 2024 — confirm this has not changed as of 2026]

san_francisco.primary_organization: Bay Area Legal Aid — San Francisco
san_francisco.verify_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Bay Area Legal Aid SF intake line as of 2026]

san_francisco.verify_secondary_phone: [VERIFY: Confirm Tenderloin Housing Clinic intake number against thclinic.org as of 2026]
san_francisco.secondary_note: Tenderloin Housing Clinic specializes in San Francisco Rent Ordinance and Just Cause eviction defenses for San Francisco tenants.
san_francisco.special_note: San Francisco has one of the strongest local tenant protection ordinances in California. The San Francisco Rent Ordinance covers most rental units built before June 13, 1979 (with exceptions) and provides extensive just-cause eviction protections, relocation assistance rights, and other tenant rights that go significantly beyond AB 1482 state law. SF tenants should always ask about the SF Rent Ordinance specifically. See city_ordinance_flags in california_eviction_data.json. [VERIFY: Confirm current SF Rent Ordinance coverage criteria as of 2026 — SF Board of Supervisors has amended the ordinance multiple times.]

default_fallback.message: Call 211 from any California phone and ask for tenant rights legal aid in your county. 211 is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, at no cost. You can also visit lawhelp.org/ca on your phone right now to find legal aid in your county.
